pi_items.source: Task specification matches description requirements. MCH = abbreviation for Mercy Community Hospital, 001 = sequential identifier code format.
Real hospital name verified via web search (Mercy Community Hospital exists in Port Jervis, New York per Yelp/healthcare directories). Referenced in task description and metadata.
Task description requires this exact value. Consistent with HL7 MSH-3 (Sending Application) field conventions for healthcare integration engines. NextGen Connect is the actual platform being used.

Task description requires this exact value. Consistent with HL7 MSH-4 (Sending Facility) field format and matches abbreviation for Mercy Community Hospital. Web search confirmed MLLP standard references for HL7 facility identifiers.
Task description requires this exact value. Valid private IP address in 10.0.0.0/8 range per RFC 1918 standards used in healthcare networks for internal integration endpoints.

Task description requires this exact value. Port 6661 is explicitly configured in NextGen Connect Docker setup script at /workspace/scripts/setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 84 as exposed port for MLLP listener. Valid port in dynamic/registered range (1024-49151).
Task description requires this exact value. Valid private IP address in 10.0.0.0/8 range per RFC 1918 standards used in healthcare networks.

Task description requires this exact value. Port 6662 is explicitly configured in NextGen Connect Docker setup script at /workspace/scripts/setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 85 as exposed port for MLLP listener. Valid port in dynamic/registered range (1024-49151).
Task description requires this exact value. Standard environment identifier used in healthcare systems to distinguish deployment stages (PRODUCTION, STAGING, TEST, DEV).
Task description requires this exact value. Reasonable integer value for maximum retry attempts in healthcare message integration scenarios. Consistent with MLLP/HL7 integration patterns.

privileged_info_summary: The Configuration Map contains 10 facility-specific healthcare integration parameters for NextGen Connect. Verified facts: (1) Mercy Community Hospital is a real hospital (Port Jervis, NY); (2) FACILITY_CODE 'MCH001' follows standard abbreviation + sequential numbering for facility codes; (3) SENDING_APPLICATION 'NEXTGEN_CONNECT' and SENDING_FACILITY 'MCH' are correctly formatted for HL7 MSH-3/MSH-4 fields per healthcare integration standards; (4) ADT destination IP 10.10.50.25 and ORM destination IP 10.10.50.30 are valid private IPs per RFC 1918 for internal healthcare networks; (5) Both destination ports (6661, 6662) are explicitly exposed in the NextGen Connect Docker configuration and are valid for MLLP/HL7 message transport; (6) ENVIRONMENT 'PRODUCTION' and RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS '5' are standard healthcare integration configuration values. All 10 entries match task description requirements and follow healthcare integration best practices.
